figures.caption: Table 1: Speed comparison for the two-body Allen-Cahn equation. FO denotes first-order (backpropagation-based) methods, ZO denotes zeroth-order (backprop-free) methods.
Table 2: Memory comparison for the two-body Allen-Cahn equation. FO denotes first-order, ZO denotes zeroth-order methods.

Figure 1: Scalability bottlenecks of existing first-order high-dimensional PDE solvers (STDE, SDGD, HTE, FOBAD) as problem dimension d grows from 5× 102 to 106. (a) GPU peak memory (MB) grows rapidly with dimension, leading to catastrophic OOM failures at extreme scales. (b) Wall-clock time (s) likewise increases sharply, revealing severe computational overhead.
